Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that 1 Identifies cases where there is a direct succession of three specific activities Approve Purchase Requisition to Create Order to Confirm Order no other events in between 2 Among those cases only keep those where the time between Create Order and Confirm Order in that sequence is greater than 5 days 3 Exclude those cases entirely from the final result 4 Return all other events from event log

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: First to find direct succession we can use window functions to look at the next events For each event in a case we can check what the next two events are

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to ensure At position i activity equals Approve Purchase Requisition At position i plus 1 activity equals Create Order At position i plus 2 activity equals Confirm Order And these are consecutive in the case which window functions like LEAD ordered by timestamp can help with

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Assuming ordering by timestamp is sufficient to establish sequence If timestamps are equal we might need a tie breaker but the prompt does not mention one

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The LEAD approach finds any occurrence where an event is followed by those two But does it guarantee that there are no other events in between Yes LEAD activity 1 is the very next event and LEAD activity 2 is the event after that So if they match the names they are directly succeeding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The timing condition time elapsed between Create Order and Confirm Order of that sequence is more than 5 days In the CTE above next timestamp 1 corresponds to Create Order and next timestamp 2 corresponds to Confirm Order So the condition is next timestamp 2 minus next timestamp 1 greater than interval 5 days

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Another approach self join Join the table with itself three times Checking no other events between in a self join is more complex and less efficient than window functions Window functions are cleaner

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If a case has multiple such sequences we only need to exclude it once That is fine with distinct case id

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If a case has any such sequence with greater than 5 days gap exclude the whole case My query does this because cases to exclude gets all case ids with at least one matching sequence and then we exclude those case ids entirely

Name: Validation - PE
Text: More than 5 days means strictly greater so greater than interval 5 days is correct

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternative to not in not exists or left join not in is fine here unless case id can be null not exists is often preferred to avoid null issues Let us use not exists to be safest

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: To be robust across timestamp and date one idea is datediff day But for elapsed time greater than 5 days using interval subtraction is more accurate so I will stick with next ts 2 minus next ts 1 greater than interval 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This looks solid The query handles any starting position of the sequence and excludes the whole case if at least one matching sequence satisfies the timing condition
